hook.text: 'When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure.' Goodhart warned us in 1975.

buildup.text: A call center targets 'calls per hour.' Agents start hanging up fast. Calls increase. Satisfaction drops.

discovery.text: Wells Fargo targeted 'accounts per customer.' Employees opened millions of fake accounts. The metric looked great.

twist.text: The fix: measure outcomes, not outputs. Target 'problems resolved' not 'calls handled' for support teams.

climax.text: In UX, targeting 'task completion rate' can push teams to simplify tasks instead of improving the product.

punchline.text: Targets get gamed. Measure outcomes, not outputs.

quiz.question: What does Goodhart's Law state about metrics?
options: When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure
All metrics improve over time naturally
Only financial metrics can be reliably tracked
